seeds.question: Can I really use Alipay or WeChat Pay with a foreign card in China?
seeds.concern: Travelers worry that payments will fail at restaurants, taxis, metro gates, and street stalls after they land.
seeds.answer_seed: Yes, most visitors should set up both Alipay and WeChat Pay before flying, complete passport verification, and link a foreign Visa, Mastercard, Amex, JCB, Discover, or Diners card. The guide should still explain where foreign-card wallets can fail: person-to-person transfers, some merchant QR codes, verification issues, bank fraud blocks, and occasional cash-only backup moments.

related_guides.title: Alipay & WeChat Pay With a Foreign Card
Didi, Metro QR Codes, Getting Around

seeds.evidence_summary: CrowdListen found three traveler reports about failed or uncertain Alipay/WeChat Pay usage, one official Alipay traveler source, and one recent secondary travel guide. The guide update now covers linked-card failures, private QR limitations, no-app fallback planning, and second-card/cash backup order.
search_phrases: can foreigners use alipay in china
do I need cash in china
seeds.codex_brief: Refresh the payment guide with the newest repeated failure modes, then add a short QA answer for each failure mode.

seeds.question: Do I need a China visa, or can I use the 240-hour visa-free transit policy?
seeds.concern: Travelers are confused by passport rules, A-China-B routing, Hong Kong/Macau/Taiwan treatment, and whether round trips qualify.
seeds.answer_seed: The answer depends on passport, route, and trip length. A normal visa-free country visitor may get up to 30 days, while US, UK, Canadian, and other non-listed passports often use the 240-hour transit route if they arrive from one country or region and leave to a different one. The guide must date every rule and push readers to verify with official sources before booking.
seeds.evidence_summary: Starter seed from current visa and itinerary FAQs. Replace with CrowdListen clusters from route-planning questions and recent traveler reports.
search_phrases: does hong kong count for china transit visa
do us citizens need china visa
seeds.codex_brief: Generate route examples and failure cases, then add dated official-source caveats.

seeds.question: How do I turn a China travel link from social media into a saved place on my map?
seeds.concern: Travelers discover restaurants, neighborhoods, hotels, tea houses, and attractions in scattered posts, but saving those places into Apple Maps or a China map app is tedious.
seeds.answer_seed: The simplest workflow is paste link, extract place, verify the place name and city, then save to Apple Maps by default. Keep Gaode/AMap as an alternate option for China-specific navigation. Sign-in should happen only when saving or syncing the user's saved-place collection.
seeds.evidence_summary: Starter seed from the current map-import feature and authentic-neighborhood guides. Replace with CrowdListen link extraction and place clusters.
search_phrases: save xiaohongshu place to apple maps
china travel social media itinerary map
seeds.codex_brief: Use CrowdListen source extraction to identify place names, city, address hints, and recommendation reason, then emit a save-ready place card.

seeds.question: Will hotels in China accept my foreign passport?
seeds.concern: Travelers hear that some hotels cannot host foreigners and worry about being rejected at check-in.
seeds.answer_seed: Most mainstream city hotels accept foreign passports, but some budget hotels, homestays, small-town properties, and older listings may not. The best answer should recommend booking platforms that expose foreigner-friendly inventory, confirming the first hotel, carrying the Chinese address, and avoiding unverified budget listings after a late arrival.
seeds.evidence_summary: Starter seed from current hotel guide FAQs. Replace with CrowdListen hotel-review and booking-thread clusters.
search_phrases: can foreigners stay in any hotel in china
seeds.codex_brief: Build a city-by-city hotel booking checklist and collect examples of rejection/acceptance language from reviews.

seeds.question: How do I order food in China if I cannot read Chinese?
seeds.concern: Travelers are blocked by QR menus, untranslated dish names, spice levels, allergies, and payment flows.
seeds.answer_seed: A practical guide should teach camera translation, screenshot translation for QR menus, pointing at visible dishes, common dietary lines in Chinese, and how to pay from the same phone. It should also route food discovery into saved places so recommendations become a trip plan.

related_guides.title: Food: QR Menus and Dietary Needs

seeds.question: Where can I see everyday China beyond the major landmarks?
seeds.concern: Travelers want authentic neighborhoods, food streets, tea houses, parks, and local routines, but do not know which recommendations are real versus tourist traps.
seeds.answer_seed: The guide should organize recommendations by place type: Beijing hutongs that still have daily life, Shanghai longtang and former French Concession side streets, Chengdu tea houses and parks, wet markets, and morning park culture. It should include etiquette: buy something, ask before photographing people, and avoid treating residential lanes as attractions.

recommended_places.name: Anfu Road and Wukang Road side streets
